schemas.name: Virtual Auditor
schemas.description: AI-powered CA and IBBI Registered Valuer firm offering IBBI-compliant valuations (ESOP, FEMA, Income Tax, Companies Act, SEBI, IBC, 409A, complex instruments, AIF), FEMA compliance, GST and income tax appeals, forensic accounting, company registration, and secretarial services across India.

mainEntity.name: What is the difference between startup valuation and business valuation?

acceptedAnswer.text: Startup valuation focuses on early-stage, often pre-revenue companies using qualitative and probability-based methods. Business valuation covers all companies — profitable, loss-making, mature, distressed — using the full range of income, market, and asset approaches. Our 18-method framework handles both.

mainEntity.name: Is IBBI registration mandatory for business valuation?

acceptedAnswer.text: For Companies Act requirements (Section 247): yes. For FEMA: CA or Merchant Banker suffices. For Income Tax (Rule 11UA): CA or Merchant Banker. For IBC: IBBI RV mandatory. Best practice: always use a Registered Valuer.

mainEntity.name: How do you value a loss-making company?

acceptedAnswer.text: Loss-making companies are valued using: DCF based on projected recovery to profitability, comparable company analysis (EV/Revenue multiples for revenue-generating but unprofitable companies), and adjusted NAV for asset-heavy businesses. The key is identifying the correct assumption about when profitability will be achieved.

mainEntity.name: What data do I need to provide?

acceptedAnswer.text: 3-5 years of audited financials, management business plan with projections, shareholding pattern, details of capital structure (including convertible instruments), industry/market data, and details of the specific transaction triggering the valuation.

M&A transactions, fundraising, partner buyouts, shareholder disputes, tax compliance (Section 56(2)(x)), FEMA share pricing, IBC proceedings, Companies Act requirements (Section 247), ESOP grants, financial reporting (Ind AS), and internal strategic planning.

mainEntity.name: How do you handle lack of comparable companies?

acceptedAnswer.text: Use comparable transactions instead of companies. Apply Total Beta (Damodaran methodology) for private company risk. Use industry regression models. Cross-validate with asset-based and income-based approaches. Monte Carlo simulation accounts for uncertainty.

mainEntity.name: What is DLOM and how do you calculate it?

acceptedAnswer.text: Discount for Lack of Marketability — adjustment for illiquidity of private company shares. We use Chaffe Put Option Model and Finnerty Model (not arbitrary percentages). Typical DLOM range: 15-35% depending on company characteristics.

mainEntity.name: Can you value distressed or loss-making companies?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. Liquidation value for distressed assets. Going concern DCF with restructuring assumptions for turnarounds. Adjusted book value for asset-heavy businesses. IBC Regulation 35 requires both fair value and liquidation value.
